drafts.text:
I type for a living, but I think faster than I type. So I built Jot — a free, open-source dictation app for Mac.

Press a hotkey, speak, and the words land wherever your cursor is — email, Slack, your code editor. No window switching, no copy-paste.

What makes it different:
• 100% on-device — transcription runs on the Apple Neural Engine. Your voice never leaves your Mac.
• $0. No subscription, no account, no telemetry.
• Optional AI cleanup and rewrite, on-device with Apple Intelligence.

The paid alternatives charge $8–15/month for this. Jot is free because dictation on a Mac shouldn't need a server.

Try it (free, Apple Silicon, macOS 14+):
drafts.note: The link preview card renders automatically — run the URL through LinkedIn's Post Inspector once before your first post so it picks up the fresh OG image.

drafts.text:
I built a free, open-source dictation app for Mac.

Hotkey → speak → text appears at your cursor. Works in any app.

100% on-device (Apple Neural Engine). No account. No subscription. No telemetry.
drafts.note: Attach a 10–15s screen recording if you have one — posts with video get ~3x the reach of plain text.

drafts.text:
Jot — free, open-source, fully on-device dictation for macOS (I built it)

I got frustrated paying monthly for Mac dictation apps while Apple's built-in dictation kept fumbling punctuation, so I built my own and open-sourced it.

Jot sits in the menu bar. Press a hotkey, talk, and the transcript pastes at your cursor in whatever app you're in. Transcription runs entirely on-device on the Neural Engine (Parakeet model), so it works offline and your audio never leaves the machine. No account, no telemetry, free.

There's optional AI cleanup/rewrite — it defaults to Apple Intelligence on-device, or you can point it at Ollama or your own API key.

Needs Apple Silicon + macOS 14+. Would love feedback, especially on the rewrite-with-voice flow.
drafts.note: Post to r/macapps (self-promo is welcome there if you engage with comments). First line is the post title, the rest is the body. Don't repost to another subreddit for at least 4–5 days.

drafts.text:
Talk instead of type. Press a hotkey, speak, and the words land at your cursor — in any app on your Mac.

Jot is free, open-source, and runs 100% on-device on the Apple Neural Engine. No account. No subscription. Your voice never leaves your Mac.

Free download — link in bio. 🎙️

#macos #dictation #opensource #productivity #speechtotext #macapps #indiedev #buildinpublic
drafts.visual: 10–15s screen recording as a Reel: press the hotkey, the pill appears under the notch, speak one sentence, the text lands in Notes. Text overlay: 'free · open source · on-device'. Screen recordings of the pill animation are the most Instagram-able thing Jot has.
drafts.note: Instagram doesn't make caption links clickable — put the UTM link in your bio (you can use the bare jot.ideaflow.page?utm_source=instagram link there permanently).

notes: Day-zero brief written by hand to seed the system; from tomorrow the 5:30am PT agent takes over with fresh angles daily plus Reddit/HN reply opportunities. Suggested order today: LinkedIn and X this morning, Reddit tomorrow morning in its window (don't launch everything in one hour — spread the first wave across 2 days so each post gets your full attention in the comments).
